author: govinda dńĀsa
verses: jaya jaga‚čÖtńĀraŠĻáa‚čÖkńĀraŠĻáa dhńĀma ńĀnanda‚čÖkanda nityńĀnanda nńĀma Šłćagamaga locana‚čÖkamala ŠłćhulńĀyata sahaje athira gati jiti matoyńĀra bhńĀiyńĀ abhirńĀma bali ghana ghana ŠłćńĀka‚čÖi gaura prema‚čÖbhare cala‚čÖi nńĀ pńĀra gada gada ńĀdhamadhura vacanńĀmŠĻõta lahu lahu hńĀsa‚čÖvikaŇõita ganta pńĀŠĻ£aŠĻáŠłća khaŠĻáŠłćana Ňõrńę‚čÖbhuja‚čÖmaŠĻáŠłćana kanaka‚čÖ khacita avalamvana daŠĻáŠłća kali‚čÖyuga kńĀlabhujaŠĻÖgama daŠĻĀŇõala dagadhala sthńĀvara jńĀŠĻÖgama dekhi prema‚čÖsudhńĀ‚čÖrasa jaga bhari barikhala govinda‚čÖdńĀsa ke kńĀŇčhe upekhi
en_translation:
All glories to the abode that is the cause of the world's deliverance, the root of all bliss, whose Name is NityńĀnanda.

His lotus-like eyes sway unsteadily with ease. His wavering gait outdoes even an intoxicated person. Calling out "O brother AbhirńĀma!" again and again, Gaura is unable to walk, overwhelmed with divine love.

Faltering, half-sweet, nectarous words gently flow from His mouth, His face blossoming with a soft smile. The destroyer of atheists, He holds a golden staff inlaid with gold, which adorns His beautiful arms.

Seeing all beings, both moving and non-moving, bitten and scorched by the serpent of the Age of Kali, He showered the entire world with the nectarean essence of divine love. Why then does He neglect Govinda DńĀsa?
